operating_state: NIKE revenues increased 1% to $12.4 billion driven by North America wholesale growth from marketplace management actions and expanded distribution, offset by NIKE Direct declines from reduced digital traffic. Greater China revenues fell on currency-neutral basis while Converse declined amid strategic brand reset. Operating cash flows decreased $642 million driven by lower net income and unfavorable working capital changes from reduced accounts payable and increased accounts receivable.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 300 basis points to 40.6% primarily from new tariffs creating $1.5 billion annualized cost impact, reducing margins by 360 basis points, with additional pressure from lower average selling prices due to channel mix. Operating expenses remained controlled with demand creation expense increasing for brand and sports marketing investments while operating overhead decreased from lower wage-related costs.
material_events: Nike appointed Venkatesh Alagirisamy as Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer effective December 2025 while eliminating the Chief Commercial Officer role with Craig Williams transitioning out by April 2026. Share repurchases were paused during Q1 fiscal 2026 due to lower operating cash flows, though the program remains authorized, while the company returned $598 million through dividends.
macro_exposures: New tariff policies created gross incremental cost of approximately $1.5 billion on annualized basis with negative impact on fiscal 2026 gross margin expected. Moody's downgraded the company's debt rating from A1 to A2 in November 2025, though facility fees and interest rates remained unchanged. Company faces ongoing geopolitical dynamics, tax regulation changes, and fluctuating foreign currency exchange rates.
forward_outlook: Management expects negative impacts from Greater China and Converse to continue throughout fiscal 2026 as these segments progress through strategic actions. Company focused on accelerating product innovation, reducing supply of certain footwear products, and increasing investment in demand creation to support key product launches. Management may resume share repurchases depending on market conditions and capital needs.
uncertainty_profile: Timing of financial impacts from strategic actions varies by segment with North America making most progress while Greater China and Converse requiring more time. Future capital market volatility may increase costs for commercial paper or debt instruments. The company maintains $8.3 billion in cash and short-term investments, believing current liquidity sufficient for domestic and foreign capital needs over the next twelve months.
